Office of the Secretary of State

Fe ruary 27, 1979 
Ms. Britta M. Skavdahl, Treasurer 
1230 J Street 
P.O. Box 1467 
Sacramento, California 95807 
The California College Republicans, IDI1790245 
10813 Escobar 
San Diego, CA 92124 
Dear Ms. Skavdahl: 
POLITICAL REFORM DIVISION 
(916) 3224880 
Thank you for filing your Recipient Committee Statement of Organization 
(Form 410), executed on February 3, 1979. This letter is to assign your committee 
identification number and to notify you of the liability for filing your statement 
after the specified deadline. 
ASSIGNMENT OF COMMITTEE IDENTIFICATION NUMBER 
Your committee's identification number is 790245. All correspondence and 
future campaign disclosure statements must include this identification number. 
YOUr committee should also advise candidates and committees to which you 
make contributions or expenditures of your identification number. 
NOTICE OF LATE FILING LIABILITY 
Form 410 requires that "date qualified as committee" be provided. Committees 
must file a Statement of Organization with our office within 10 days of the 
date on which they qualify. 
Explanation of "Date Qualified" 
"Date qualified" refers to the date on which your committee received 
contributions totalling $500 or more during the calendar year. If your 
committee did not qualify on [he date you indicated, you should complete 
the enclosed Fonn 405 (Amendment to Campaign Disclosure Statement) 
and report the exact date (day, month, year) on which your committee 
qualified. 
--
If your committee has not yet qualified but needs a committee identification 
number, you should complete the enclosed Form 405 and indicate that 
the committee has not yet qualified. IT IS NOT NECESSARY TO FILE 
ANOTHER STATEMENT OF ORGANIZATION WHEN THE COMMITTEE 
DOES QUALIFY. 
According to the date you indicated on your Statement of Organization, you 
did not, as required by law, file your statement within ten days of qualification 
and therefore you have been fined. 


Britta 
Skavdahl 
February 27, L 979 
Page 2 
Committees filing a statement after a specified deadline are liable in the amount 
of $10 per day for each day late, up to a maximum fine of $100. YOU HAVE 
INCURRED A LIABILITY OF $100.00. The Political Reform Act permits waiver 
of the liability in certain limited cases. If your Statement of Organization 
was filed late, you may complete the enclosed "Request for Waiver". We will 
notify you of the results of your request. 
If you have any questions pertaining to this letter, please contact this office. 
If your questions are of an interpretive nature, please contact the Technical 
Assistance and Analysis Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission 
at (916) 322-5662 in Sacramento or (213) 620-5196 in Los Angeles. 
Sincerely, 
WILLIAM N. DURLEY 
Assistant to the Secretary of State 
Elections and Political Reform 
I 
.A? 
G~J:R. MORROW, Auditor 
Political Reform Division 
cc: Fair Political Practices Commission

(8) STATE IN FULL ON REVERSE SIDE THE REASON WHY THE STATEMENT WAS

I declare and certify under penalty of perjury that the foregoing information on this 
request for waiver is true and correct. I hereby request that the liability for failing 
to file a statement required by the Political Reform Act on time be waived.

sections.question_synthetic: The California College Republicans submitted a late Statement of Organization (Form 410) and are requesting waiver of the $100 fine incurred for filing after the 10-day deadline, arguing they were unaware a committee identification number could be obtained without mandatory filing.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document is primarily an incoming request for waiver and supporting forms rather than an FPPC advice letter. Contains late filing notification letter from Secretary of State and waiver request form. Quality of OCR varies; some text fragments are degraded. Document ID provided (79-017) matches 1979 date. No formal FPPC question and conclusion sections present.

embedding.summary: This document package contains a Statement of Organization (Form 410) filed by the California College Republicans on February 3, 1979, along with official correspondence from the Secretary of State's Elections and Political Reform Division dated February 27, 1979 notifying them of late filing liability ($100 fine), and a Request for Waiver of Liability submitted by Hunt Braly on March 21, 1979.
